title: senecio halophilus
intro: Senecio halophilus, also known as the saltwort or sea cotton-thistle, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region and coastal areas of Europe, Africa, and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and diuretic effects, which can help relieve respiratory issues and reduce fluid retention. The health benefits of Senecio halophilus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its ability to support the body's natural detoxification processes. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
